title: Pipeline enforcement via Claude Code hooks
description: Two Claude Code hooks that mechanically enforce pipeline constraints. (1) Stop hook: reads checkpoint file at completion, validates required agents were spawned, review artifacts exist, digest was written. Derives requirements from directive weight — lightweight passes trivially. (2) Scope enforcement: PreToolUse hook in Alex's agent frontmatter blocks Edit/Write to source code, forcing delegation to engineer agents. Replaces 15+ ALWAYS/NEVER prompt rules with mechanical enforcement. ~130 lines of bash total.

in: Stop hook (~100 lines bash): read checkpoint, derive requirements from weight, check reviewers spawned + audit done + digest written + phases completed
Scope enforcement hook (~30 lines bash): PreToolUse deny on Edit|Write for non-.context/ non-.claude/ paths in Alex's frontmatter
Add enforcement sub-object to checkpoint schema: agents_spawned, review_artifacts_written
Update checkpoint.sh to support the new enforcement fields
Remove ALWAYS/NEVER rules from pipeline docs that are now mechanically enforced
Hook placement: Stop + scope in alex-cos.md frontmatter (orchestrator-only)
out: Per-step validation hooks (PostToolUse on every Read/Write) — over-engineered for current scale
Separate enforcement state file — checkpoint already tracks what we need
State file tamper protection — not worth the complexity
SubagentStart/SubagentStop tracking hooks — checkpoint covers this
Morgan plan JSON validation hook — Alex can just retry the parse
Digest structure validation hook — Stop hook checks existence is enough
TypeScript constraint checker script — bash hooks are simpler and external
Constraint definition format / escalation ladder — replaced by direct hook logic
correction-to-code-pipeline — depends on constraint system we're not building

dod.criterion: Stop hook blocks Alex from completing when no reviewer agents were spawned for initiatives that require review
Stop hook blocks when no digest was written to .context/reports/
Stop hook passes trivially for lightweight directives (no review/audit required)
Scope enforcement blocks Alex from writing to source code files
Scope enforcement allows Alex to write to .context/ and .claude/ paths
Checkpoint schema includes enforcement fields (agents_spawned, review_artifacts)
At least 5 ALWAYS/NEVER rules removed from pipeline docs (replaced by hooks)

tasks.title: Build Stop hook — master completion validator
tasks.scope: Write .claude/hooks/enforce-completion.sh (~100 lines). Read checkpoint file, derive requirements from weight in directive.json (case statement: lightweight=no review required, medium+=auditor+reviewer+digest, heavyweight+=challenger+plan-for-approval). Check agents_spawned, review artifacts, digest existence. Exit 2 to block, exit 0 to allow. Include second-attempt passthrough to prevent infinite loops.

tasks.title: Add enforcement fields to checkpoint schema + update checkpoint.sh
tasks.scope: Add enforcement sub-object to checkpoint.json schema: { agents_spawned: [], review_artifacts_written: [] }. Add checkpoint.sh subcommands: track-agent <directive> <agent-type>, track-artifact <directive> <path>. Update pipeline docs to call these after agent spawns and review completions.

tasks.title: Build scope enforcement hook — block Alex from writing source code
tasks.scope: Write .claude/hooks/enforce-orchestrator-scope.sh (~30 lines). PreToolUse hook for Edit|Write. Allow .context/, .claude/, ~/.claude/ paths. Deny everything else with clear message: 'Orchestrator cannot modify source code. Spawn an engineer agent.' Add to alex-cos.md frontmatter hooks section.

tasks.title: Remove ALWAYS/NEVER rules replaced by hooks from pipeline docs
tasks.scope: Grep pipeline docs for ALWAYS/NEVER rules. Remove ones now mechanically enforced (review required, audit required, digest required, don't write source code). Keep rules that hooks can't enforce (include personality files, include verify command, etc). Count before/after.
